title: Reiva Carrick
content:

{{Quote|I assure you, madam, your son could not be safer. After all, we're not just their teachers… we're their ''protectors''<nowiki></nowiki>.|Vandar Tokare, to Reiva Carrick|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
'''Reiva Carrick''', a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], was a gardener from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Phaeda/Legends|Phaeda]] and the [[Parent/Legends|mother]] of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]]. Reiva was [[Marriage/Legends|married]] to a [[Banker/Legends|banker]] named [[Arvan Carrick]], with whom she had five children, Zayne being the only son. In [[3977 BBY]], the five-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old Zayne, who turned out to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], was referred to the [[Jedi Enclave]] on the planet [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. Although the boy's [[The Force/Legends|Force]] affinity proved to be marginal, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vandar Tokare]] convinced Reiva that her son would be safe in the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. In [[3964 BBY]], Zayne was accused of [[Padawan Massacre|murdering]] four other Padawans in his class, but neither Reiva nor the rest of her [[Carrick family|family]] believed the charges.

==Biography==
{{Quote|But is the order a safe place for a child? Those ''dark'' Jedi—are they gone for good?|Reiva Carrick, to Vandar Tokare|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], Reiva Carrick was a native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Phaeda/Legends|Phaeda]],<ref name="handbook" /> located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="tea">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> A gardener,<ref name="kotor8">[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]]</ref> she [[Marriage/Legends|married]] a [[Banker/Legends|banker]] named [[Arvan Carrick]], with whom she [[Reproduction/Legends|bore]] five children—[[Zayne Carrick|Zayne]], their only son, was born in [[3982 BBY]]. [[3977 BBY|Just after]] Zayne's fifth [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]], the child, discovered to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], was referred to the [[Jedi Enclave]] on the planet [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].<ref name="handbook" /> The [[Unidentified Jedi Knight (Zayne Carrick)|Jedi Knight]] who suggested Zayne be taken to Dantooine told Reiva that the boy only had a marginal proclivity to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Although this was confirmed on Dantooine by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vandar Tokare]], the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] were impressed by Zayne's ability to form kinships with the other [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] at the Enclave. The Jedi's examination of young Zayne took a full morning—after it was over, Reiva sought Tokare out, hoping to learn more about her son's prospects. Although Reiva was unsure whether the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] was a safe place for her child, remembering the events of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] some [[Time/Legends|time]] before, Tokare assured her that Zayne would be free from danger with the Jedi.<ref name="kotor2" />

In [[3964 BBY]], Zayne, now a Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] in [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] on the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], was accused of [[Padawan Massacre|murdering]] four of his classmates.<ref name="kotor2" /> Zayne went on the run, and word of his alleged crimes eventually reached the [[Carrick family]]. Reiva and her husband did not believe that their son would do such a thing, although it became difficult at home. A few [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] later, Arvan was transferred to the major corporate world of [[Telerath/Legends|Telerath]]—Reiva and Arvan believed that the opportunity had to do with the news about their son, and they moved to Telerath along with their four daughters. In [[3963 BBY]], Arvan was kidnapped by the [[Moomo Brothers]], a pair of [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], but was saved by Zayne, who happened to be on Telerath at the time. After the ordeal, Reiva, Arvan and their daughters left Telerath and moved to Dantooine, where Tokare welcomed them with open arms at the Jedi Enclave.<ref name="kotor12">[[Knights of the Old Republic 12|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 12]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''We'' never asked. We know you didn't do it.|Arvan Carrick, to his son, Zayne|<ref name="kotor12" />}}
In her son Zayne's youth, Reiva Carrick was very protective of her little boy and was concerned for his safety if he were to join the Jedi Order. However, she had her fears assuaged by Master Vandar Tokare.<ref name="kotor2" /> Just like her husband, Carrick refused to believe that Zayne was responsible for the deaths of the other Padawans on Taris, knowing it to be out of his character.<ref name="kotor12" /> Carrick had light skin and [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]].<ref name="kotor2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Reiva Carrick was first indirectly mentioned in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] [[Knights of the Old Republic 2|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 2]], which was written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and released by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="kotor1">[[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 1]]</ref> on [[January 25]], [[2006]].<ref name="DH">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-857/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-1|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #1}}</ref> She first appeared in a flashback in the [[Knights of the Old Republic 2|following issue]], which was also written by [[John Jackson Miller]], illustrated by [[Brian Ching]],<ref name="kotor2" /> and published on [[February 22]] of the same year.<ref name="DH1">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-858/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-2|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #2}}</ref>
